unit: Introduction: The End of the Essay?
assignment_1: Read this syllabus
Read Hsu, “The End of the Essay”
assignment_2: Read “Often helpful, sometimes bad, always dangerous”

assignment_1: In-class writing workshop
Ask a chatbot “What are the benefits of reference managers for university students?”
assignment_2: Read *The Chicago Manual of Style*, sections 13.1 (“The purpose of source citations”), 13.5 (“Mentioning versus citing”), and 13.3 (“Citation management tools”
assignment_n: Watch [How to use Zotero](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JG7Uq_JFDzE) and install Zotero on your machine

assignment_1: In-class writing workshop: inserting citations in a Word file with Zotero
Ask a chatbot “What are the advantages of the Zettelkasten method of note-taking for university students?”
assignment_2: Read *The Craft of Research*, sections 4.6 (“Taking Notes Systematically”) and 4.7 (“Annotating Your Sources”)
assignment_n: Explore AlternativeTo’s list of [Free Note Taking Apps with support for Markdown](https://alternativeto.net/category/productivity/note-taking/?feature=markdown-support&license=free)

assignment_1: Watch [MIT Sloan's Rama Ramakrishnan Shares Primer on ChatGPT](https://mitsloanedtech.mit.edu/ai/basics/how-chatgpt-works-a-non-technical-primer/)
assignment_2: Read Mollick, “15 Times to use AI, and 5 Not to”
assignment_n: Read “A.I. is getting more powerful, but its hallucinations are getting worse”

assignment_1: In-class writing workshop: taking notes
Ask a chatbot “What are the advantages of Markdown over Microsoft Word or Google Docs for university students?”
assignment_n: Read the [Markdown Guide](https://www.markdownguide.org/getting-started/) and experiment with Dillinger, the online Markdown editor recommended there

assignment_1: Watch [“What’s a bib file?”](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JF9bvYmcdmY&list=PLXt-tu7G1H3tLeZgbbUmYjE0_kvbjA4YU&index=2) and [“Pandoc: Academic Writing in Plain Text”](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N31E_NZYQQY)
assignment_2: Read “Getting started with pandoc” and install pandoc on your machine
assignment_n: Explore AlternativeTo’s list of [Free Text Editors with support for Markdown](https://alternativeto.net/category/productivity/text-editor/?feature=markdown-support&license=free)

assignment_1: In-class writing workshop: writing and editing in Markdown
Ask a chatbot “What are the benefits of the git version control system for university students?”
assignment_2: Watch [“A brief introduction to Git for beginners”](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r8jQ9hVA2qs&t=542s)
assignment_n: Read the lessons “About GitHub and Git”, “Creating an account with GitHub” and “Hello World” in the tutorial [Start your journey](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/start-your-journey)

assignment_1: Watch [Git, GitHub, & GitHub Desktop for beginners](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8Dd7KRpKeaE)
In-class writing workshop: editing Markdown in a git repository; adding Markdown document structure
